posts.content:
Love is not something we fall into. It's something we choose—and keep choosing—through care, presence, and the courage to show up.

— Inspired by M. Scott Peck

"To begin by always thinking of love as an action rather than a feeling is one way in which anyone using the word in this manner automatically assumes accountability."

— bell hooks

You cannot pour from an empty cup. Self-love isn't selfish—it's the foundation that makes all other love possible.

— Inspired by bell hooks

Today's practice: When someone speaks, listen fully. Don't plan your response. Just be present.

That's love in action.

— Inspired by Nonviolent Communication

"Knowing that we can be loved exactly as we are gives us all the best opportunity for growing into the healthiest of people."

— Fred Rogers

Love asks us to be curious instead of certain. To listen instead of fix. To witness instead of judge.

— Inspired by Byron Katie

What's one small way you can show yourself care today?

Not earned. Not conditional. Just because you exist.

"You yourself, as much as anybody in the entire universe, deserve your love and affection."

Start there. Everything else follows.

— Buddha
posts.source: Buddha, as quoted by Sharon Salzberg

posts.content:
Today's practice: Ask someone "How are you?" and truly listen. Don't rush to fix or advise. Just witness.

That's care.

— Inspired by NVC

Trust isn't built in grand gestures. It's built in small moments—showing up when you said you would, keeping the confidence someone shared.

— Inspired by Brené Brown

Honesty without compassion is cruelty. Compassion without honesty is manipulation. Love holds both—truth spoken with care.

— Inspired by Marshall Rosenberg

To respect someone is to see them as they truly are—not who you wish they were, not who they used to be. Just who they are, right now.

— Inspired by Erich Fromm

"Love is an act of endless forgiveness, a tender look which becomes a habit."

Commitment is love repeated daily.

— Peter Ustinov

What do you know about yourself that you're afraid to admit?

Love begins with the courage to see ourselves clearly—shadow and light.

— Inspired by John Bradshaw
